name: Shades of Green
subtitle: Walt Disney World Resort, Orlando, FL

location.address: 1905 W Magnolia Palm Dr, Lake Buena Vista, FL 32830

description: The only hotel located inside the Walt Disney World Resort complex exclusively for the military. Positioned between the Magic Kingdom and EPCOT, with free Disney transportation included. Rooms are drastically cheaper than civilian Disney resorts. Perhaps the single highest-value military benefit for families — Disney-level location at a fraction of the cost.

rates.note: Lowest available rate tier. Standard and deluxe rooms.
Standard rates for mid-to-senior active duty.
All military retirees and their families.

rates.tier: Cat IV — DoD Civilians / Disabled Vets
rates.note: DoD GS/WG employees and service-connected disabled vets.

savings_estimate: $165–$660+ per night vs. comparable civilian Disney resort

booking.window_note: 18 months in advance for all eligibility categories

cancellation_policy: 72-hour cancellation for standard dates; peak holiday periods may require 7-day notice. Deposit required for stays during Disney holidays.
amenities: Free Walt Disney World Transportation (buses to all 4 parks, Disney Springs)
7 Restaurants and Bars (Mangino's Italian, Garden Gallery, Evergreens, more)
Military Disney Salute Ticket Sales at Front Desk
Disney Early Theme Park Entry (30 min before public)
Golf Privileges at Disney Golf Courses
room_types: Standard Room (2 Queen Beds) — 314 sq ft
Standard Room (1 King Bed) — 314 sq ft
Deluxe Room (Pool/Garden View, 2 Queens) — 385 sq ft
Suite (Parlor Suite) — 630 sq ft
Triple Room (3 Double Beds) — 463 sq ft
pet_policy: No pets allowed. Service animals permitted.
parking: Free self-parking for registered guests. Valet not available.
transportation: Free Disney Bus service to Magic Kingdom, EPCOT, Hollywood Studios, Animal Kingdom, Disney Springs, and water parks. Walk or bus to Polynesian and Grand Floridian for monorail access.
tips: Book at exactly your 18-month window — popular dates (spring break, summer, Thanksgiving, Christmas) sell out within hours of opening.
Military Disney Salute tickets (4-day and 6-day discounted) sold at the Shades of Green front desk. These are frequently the cheapest Disney park tickets available.
The resort is not located near Disney Springs or Disney's Hollywood Studios bus stops, but dedicated buses run from the front of the resort.
Request a pool-view or resort-view room when booking — no upcharge but not guaranteed without request.
Dining reservations for Disney restaurants can be made 60 days out via My Disney Experience app — book immediately after securing your room.
notes: Operated by the U.S. Army MWR. All room rates subject to Florida state taxes. Rates adjust periodically — verify current pricing at ShadesOfGreen.org.

name: Hale Koa Hotel
subtitle: Waikiki Beach, Honolulu, Hawaii
description: Hale Koa ('House of the Warrior') sits directly on Waikiki Beach — some of the world's most expensive real estate — and is exclusively for the military. Five tiered eligibility categories determine your room rate, with enlisted active duty receiving the lowest prices. Civilian beachfront Waikiki hotels charge $400–$900+/night; military rates run a fraction of that for the same beachfront access.

rates.note: Mountain or city view. Best available rate.

rates.tier: Cat II — E6+ and all Officers (Active Duty)
rates.note: Standard military rates, all active duty above E5.
Activated RC members with orders.
All military retirees regardless of grade. Lower room availability than Cats I–III.
GS/WG government employees. Least availability.

civilian_comparison: Civilian Waikiki beachfront hotels (Hilton Hawaiian Village, Moana Surfrider): $400–$900+/night
savings_estimate: $200–$700+ per night vs. comparable civilian Waikiki beachfront

booking.window_note: 60 days out for Active Duty; 30 days for Retirees and DoD

cancellation_policy: 72-hour cancellation; deposit required for peak season (Christmas, New Year's, summer). Peak periods may require 7-day notice.
amenities: Nightly Sunset Luau (additional charge, book early)
Beach Equipment Rental (surfboards, bodyboards, snorkel gear)
room_types: Superior Room (City/Mountain View, 1 King or 2 Doubles)
Ocean View Room (Partial Ocean View, 1 King or 2 Doubles)
Deluxe Ocean View (Full Ocean View, 1 King or 2 Doubles)
Ocean Front Room (Direct oceanfront, 1 King)
Junior Suite (Ocean View, Separate Sitting Area)
Suite (Full Suite, Oceanfront)
pet_policy: No pets. Service animals permitted with documentation.
parking: Limited free self-parking on-site. Waikiki parking is extremely limited; recommend minimal car use. Bus and rideshare recommended for the island.
transportation: Walking distance to major Waikiki attractions, International Marketplace, and public bus routes. Free beach towel service included.
tips: The Sunset Luau sells out weeks in advance — book immediately after securing your room reservation.
Oceanfront and deluxe ocean view rooms go fast even within the booking window. Call for specific room requests rather than relying solely on online booking.
The booking window is shorter than other AFRCs (60/30 days) — set a calendar reminder.
Beach chairs and umbrellas are free but first-come first-served starting at 8 AM.
Cat I enlisted members see the biggest absolute savings — over $700/night versus comparable civilian hotels during peak season.
notes: Operated by the U.S. Army MWR. Hawaii state taxes apply. Room rates subject to change — verify at HaleKoa.com. The Luau is separately ticketed and not included in the room rate.

name: New Sanno Hotel
subtitle: Central Tokyo (Hiroo / Roppongi), Japan
description: The New Sanno is a U.S. military hotel operated by the U.S. Navy in one of Tokyo's most desirable neighborhoods — Hiroo, steps from Roppongi, Azabu-Juban, and Shibuya. The property functions as a full-service American hotel with Japanese convenience, providing a base from which to explore all of Tokyo. Comparable civilian Tokyo hotels in this location charge $350–$700/night.

rates.note: All active duty ranks. Best availability.
All military retirees. Subject to availability after Active Duty booking window.

rates.tier: Cat III — DoD Civilians and Reserve
rates.note: GS/WG DoD employees and Reserve/Guard on orders.

civilian_comparison: Comparable Tokyo luxury hotels (Westin, ANA Intercontinental, Conrad) in Minato-ku: $350–$700+/night
savings_estimate: $150–$500+ per night vs. comparable Tokyo hotels

booking.window_note: 60 days for Active Duty; 30 days for Retirees and DoD Civilians

cancellation_policy: 48–72 hours prior to arrival. Peak periods (Golden Week, Obon, New Year) may require 7-day notice and deposit.
amenities: American-Style Restaurant (full breakfast, lunch, dinner menu)
Free Shuttle to Hiroo Station (Hibiya Line, access to all Tokyo lines)
room_types: Standard Room (1 King or 2 Doubles)
Deluxe Room (Larger footprint, 1 King)
Junior Suite
Suite
pet_policy: No pets. Service animals with documentation.
parking: Very limited on-site parking. Central Tokyo driving not recommended — use subway (Hiroo Station: 5-min walk or free shuttle).
transportation: Free shuttle to Hiroo Station (Hibiya Line). From Hiroo: Shibuya in ~10 min, Ginza in ~15 min, Ueno/Akihabara in ~25 min. IC card (Suica/Pasmo) purchasable at any station.
tips: Retirees must call during Japanese business hours — the reservation staff does not operate 24/7 and the DSN line can be tricky from CONUS. Try the CIV international line.
Golden Week (late April – early May) and Cherry Blossom season (late March – early April) book out immediately — plan months ahead.
The hotel is walking distance from the U.S. Embassy and has good access to Azabu-Juban, one of Tokyo's most charming neighborhoods.
CAC or military ID required at check-in — no exceptions.
The on-site restaurant serves American comfort food at American prices, which is a relief after a day of paying Tokyo restaurant prices.
notes: Operated by Commander, Navy Region Japan (CNRJ). Japanese consumption tax applies. The hotel is technically on U.S. military leased property but operates like a hotel — no base access badge needed to check in.

name: Dragon Hill Lodge
subtitle: Yongsan Garrison, Seoul, South Korea

location.address: Yongsan Garrison, Seoul, Republic of Korea 04383

description: Dragon Hill Lodge is a full-service resort hotel on historic Yongsan Garrison in the heart of Seoul — far beyond typical base lodging. The property features a full spa, bowling center, indoor pool, multiple restaurants, and gaming facilities. Yongsan Garrison's central Seoul location puts you minutes from Itaewon, Gangnam, and Gyeongbok Palace. Comparable civilian Seoul luxury hotels run $300–$600+/night.

rates.note: Lowest tier. Garden or standard view.

rates.tier: Cat II — Active Duty E7–O10 and Warrant Officers
rates.note: Standard and superior rooms available.
Subject to availability after active duty booking window.

rates.tier: Cat IV — DoD Civilians and Reserve on Orders
rates.note: Least availability. Early booking strongly recommended.

civilian_comparison: Comparable Seoul hotels (Lotte, Grand Hyatt Seoul, Park Hyatt Gangnam): $280–$600+/night
savings_estimate: $125–$450+ per night vs. comparable Seoul luxury hotels

booking.window_note: 60 days for Active Duty; 45 days for Retirees; 30 days for DoD Civilians

cancellation_policy: 72 hours prior to arrival. Deposits required during Korean holidays (Chuseok, Lunar New Year) and peak summer season.
amenities: Shuttle Service to Itaewon and Downtown Seoul
room_types: Standard Room (1 King or 2 Doubles, 300 sq ft)
Superior Room (Upgraded furnishings, 320 sq ft)
Junior Suite (Separate living area, 450 sq ft)
Suite (Full suite with dining area, 600 sq ft)
Ondol Room (Traditional Korean floor sleeping option)
pet_policy: No pets. Service animals with advance notice and documentation.
parking: Ample on-garrison parking available. Access via Yongsan Garrison main gate — authorized patrons only. Itaewon is a 5-minute walk outside the garrison gate.
transportation: Itaewon Station (Line 6, Seoul Metro) is a 10-minute walk through the garrison. Gangnam and central Seoul accessible in 20–35 minutes by subway.
tips: KATUSA Snack Bar on Yongsan Garrison serves high-quality Korean-American fusion food at exceptional prices — one of the best dining values in all of Seoul.
Itaewon shopping district (leather goods, custom tailoring, international restaurants) is a 10-minute walk from the Lodge gate.
Book R&R stays well in advance — the Lodge is extremely popular for USFK-stationed personnel on leave.
The DMZ is 45 minutes north by car or on organized MWR tours — a one-of-a-kind experience worth planning around.
Dragon Hill is one of the most genuinely resort-like military lodging options in the world — worth a long weekend stay for any OCONUS traveler routing through Seoul.
notes: Operated by IMCOM Korea. Korean value-added tax applies. Yongsan Garrison is transitioning some functions to Camp Humphreys (Pyeongtaek) — verify current garrison access requirements before travel.

name: Edelweiss Lodge and Resort
subtitle: Garmisch-Partenkirchen, Bavaria, Germany
description: AFRC's European flagship resort sits in the heart of the Bavarian Alps in Garmisch-Partenkirchen — the same village that hosted the 1936 Winter Olympics. Steps from Germany's highest peak (the Zugspitze at 9,718 ft), the resort offers ski-in/ski-out access in winter and world-class hiking in summer. By far the most cost-effective way to experience a full Alpine resort. Comparable civilian Garmisch hotels run $250–$600+/night.

rates.note: Best room selection and lowest rates.
Standard and deluxe rooms available.
All grades. 60-day advance booking.

rates.tier: Cat IV — DoD Civilians and RC on Orders
rates.note: Most limited availability. Book immediately at window.

civilian_comparison: Comparable Garmisch Alpine resorts (Zugspitz Resort, Edelweiss Berchtesgaden): $250–$600+/night
savings_estimate: $110–$460+ per night vs. comparable civilian Alpine resort

booking.window_note: 90 days for Active Duty; 60 days for Retirees and DoD Civilians

cancellation_policy: 7 days for peak ski season (Jan–Mar) and summer high season (Jul–Aug); 72 hours for shoulder season. Full deposit required for Christmas and New Year's.
amenities: Ski-In / Ski-Out Access to Zugspitz Arena Ski Resort
Outdoor Heated Pool (open year-round with Zugspitze views)
4 Restaurants (The Summit Club, Zugspitz Grille, Alpenglow, Lobby Bar)
Cultural Day-Trip Programs (Neuschwanstein, Munich, Salzburg, Innsbruck)
Ski and Snowboard Equipment Rental On-Site
Shuttle to Garmisch Town Center and Train Station
room_types: Standard Room (Mountain or Garden View, 1 King or 2 Doubles — 320 sq ft)
Deluxe Room (Mountain View, Upgraded Furnishings — 360 sq ft)
Junior Suite (Separate Sitting Area, Mountain View — 500 sq ft)
Signature Suite (Full Suite, Panoramic Zugspitze View — 750 sq ft)
Bunk Bed Room (Family-Oriented, Sleeps 4)
pet_policy: Small pets permitted in designated pet-friendly rooms with advance notice and a fee. Service animals always welcome.
parking: Free on-site parking. Easy car access to Bavarian Alps day-trip destinations.
transportation: Free shuttle to Garmisch-Partenkirchen train station (Zugspitzbahn, Bayerische Zugspitzbahn). Munich accessible by train in ~90 minutes. Innsbruck, Austria is 45 minutes by car.
tips: Peak ski season (January–March) and summer peak (July–August) book out the day the 90-day window opens — set a phone alarm.
Mountain-view rooms command the same rate as garden-view — request specifically when booking. The view of the Zugspitze from the outdoor pool is the signature image of this resort.
Neuschwanstein Castle day trip is coordinated by on-site MWR staff and is one of the best-value tours in Europe. Typically runs €30–€60 per person.
The Zugspitze cable car (Seilbahn) departs from town — not from the resort — but the ski area has ski-in/ski-out access via the slopes.
This is one of the best-value military benefits in the world for families who enjoy the outdoors. A week here during ski season at Cat I rates costs less than two nights at a comparable Swiss resort.
notes: Operated by IMCOM Europe. German VAT applies to room rates. Ski lift tickets and equipment rental are separately priced but available at steep discounts through the on-site rental shop compared to civilian lift ticket windows.
